rationale: With Novaris holding 11.6% of Tethyan territory and military balance favoring them, reinforcing defensive positions is critical to prevent further advances despite recent battlefield successes.
A show of maritime strength in the Black Sea deters further aggression and complements land-based defensive consolidation amid high crisis levels.
Precision strikes on rear-line logistics exploit enemy overextension and economic strain, degrading sustainment under current war costs.
With crisis at 8.0 and enemy economic contraction underway, covert sabotage of Novaris’ military logistics amplifies strain and disrupts troop resupply.
Targeting Novaris’ defense infrastructure exploits battlefield friction and intelligence opportunities revealed by recent air incident and economic disruptions.
Undermine domestic morale in Novaris by amplifying narratives of military overreach and economic collapse, leveraging real economic strain for psychological effect.
With sanctions at 52% and diminishing returns on further punitive measures, securing critical energy and food reserves insulates Tethys from escalating economic warfare.
Mobilize domestic capital to offset GDP contraction and fund defense spending, leveraging 64% international support to boost investor confidence.
Coordinate with allies to tighten restrictions on dual-use exports, exploiting Novaris’ economic contraction and high war costs to degrade long-term military capacity.
Given our unfavorable military balance and 11.6% territory loss, reinforcing defensive positions is essential to prevent further advances and consolidate gains from recent battlefield success.
Increased patrol activity will deter enemy probing operations and provide early warning of new offensives, leveraging our improved morale after key defensive victories.

Disabling command-and-control systems in their rear echelons will exploit battlefield momentum and degrade coordination during high-tempo operations.

With international support at 64%, now is the time to institutionalize aid and coordinate next-phase responses, including potential no-fly zone enforcement.
While maintaining public resolve, discreet channels can probe for fissures within the aggressor’s leadership amid growing economic pain and battlefield setbacks.
Establishing monitored corridors strengthens our moral standing and pressures Novaris diplomatically, especially as civilian hardship grows under sanctions.
With sanctions already at 52% and six months of reserves remaining, securing food, fuel, and medical supplies is critical to sustain domestic stability and war economy resilience.
Launching a national war bond campaign will mobilize internal capital, reduce reliance on foreign aid, and signal long-term economic endurance to both allies and adversaries.
Coordinate with coalition partners to tighten restrictions on dual-use exports, exploiting their GDP contraction to further limit military sustainment capacity.
